title: The hidden system behind every Renaissance trial - Ada Palmer
summary: Dwarkesh Patel interviews Ada Palmer about systematic patterns underlying Renaissance trials. The discussion explores hidden institutional and procedural systems that governed how trials operated during the Renaissance period.
writeup: Dwarkesh Patel interviews historian Ada Palmer about the institutional and procedural systems that governed how trials operated during the Renaissance. The conversation focuses on the recurring patterns underlying these trials rather than individual cases, arguing that consistent structures, not isolated events, shaped how justice was administered in the period. The takeaway is that Renaissance trials followed a hidden system of institutional and procedural rules worth examining on its own terms.
